title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Know About Accidental Disability Benefits in Term Insurance
A term insurance policy gives financial security in the case of a premature demise. This means that if you pass away during the policy tenure, the policy will give your family financial assistance. But what if there is a disability instead? Do you get any term insurance benefits?
Disabilities or disablements are challenging physiological trauma that also involves a financial loss. If you cannot work or lose your job, your family suffers an equal financial loss, as in the case of your premature demise. Moreover, disabilities might also involve modifications to your home or vehicle for your changed lifestyle. This involves further expenses. That is why disabilities are contingencies that need a financial provision.
In a vanilla term insurance plan, disabilities are not covered. However, term life insurance plans allow you to add disability coverage through an optional rider. Let’s understand.
The accidental disability rider
The accidental disability rider is an optional rider that term plans offer. This rider covers you against disabilities suffered due to an accident. Coverage is available for –
Permanent total disablement
Permanent partial disablement
Temporary total disablement
Who can avail this rider?
Coverage for the accidental disability rider is available only if you are 18 years and above. Minors cannot avail of the cover. Moreover, there might be a maximum age up to which coverage might be allowed.
Types of disabilities covered by insurers
The rider pays a benefit if there is a disability suffered due to an accident. The disability should occur within a maximum of 180 days after an accident. The types of disabilities and their respective benefits are as follows –
Permanent total disablement
A permanent total disablement happens in any of the following instances –
Loss of both limbs
Loss of one hand and one leg
Loss of vision in both eyes
In these instances, 100% of the rider sum insured. For instance, if the rider sum insured is Rs.20 lakhs, the benefit payable would be Rs.20 lakhs.
Permanent partial disablement
A permanent and partial disablement is considered in the following instances –
Loss of one hand or one leg
Loss of vision in one eye
Partial dismemberment
In these instances, a part of the rider sum insured is paid depending on the severity of the disablement. The benefit can range from 5% to 95% of the sum insured, depending on the disability suffered.
Temporary total disablement
A temporary disablement is when the disablement is not permanent, i.e., you can recover in a few weeks. Examples include fractures, burns, etc.
In the case of these disablements, a percentage of the rider sum insured is paid weekly up to a specified maximum number of weeks.
Claim procedure
To make a claim for the accidental disability rider, the process is as follows –
Inform the insurance company immediately. Fill up the rider claim form detailing the accident and the nature of the disability
Submit the form along with the relevant claim-related documents
The insurer would assess your claim form, verify the documents and pay the claim
Documents required
The documents required for a successful claim settlement for the accidental disability benefit are as follows –
Policy bond
Claim form, filled and signed
Police FIR, in the case of road accidents or other accidents where the FIR is needed
All medical reports and diagnostic tests
Identity proof of the insured member
Disability certificate issued by a registered medical practitioner
Why is the rider important?
The accidental disability benefit rider is beneficial because it adds a layer of protection to your term plan. The benefits of the rider are as follows –
Lump sum benefit
The rider pays a lump sum benefit on the occurrence of a disability. You can use this benefit to avail yourself of advanced medical treatments, make the necessary modifications in your lifestyle, pay for your financial obligations, or compensate for the financial loss that you suffer.
Financial security
The rider gives financial assistance for disabilities, which also involves a considerable financial loss. The disability rider, thus, ensures complete financial security even when the insured does not die but becomes disabled following an accident.
Tax benefit
The premium paid towards the rider qualifies for a deduction under Section 80C. Thus, your additional premium would lower your tax liability even further.
So, make your term life insurance plan comprehensive with the addition of the accidental disability rider. Enjoy the maximum term insurance benefits and stay protected even with disabilities.
